name: Fitascetta (Onion Covered Bread Ring)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Red onions, sliced very fine
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir the yeast into the water in a large mixing bowl and let stand until it is creamy, about 10 minutes.
2. With a wooden spoon, stir in the olive oil followed by the flour and salt. Knead by hand until moist and velvety.
3. Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled.
4. Meanwhile, sauté the onions in margarine over the lowest possible heat until they are soft and transparent, about 40 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and sugar.
5. Leave to cool.
6. When the dough has risen, turn out onto a floured board and shape into a long log. Transfer to a greased baking sheet and join the two ends to make a ring of 6–8 inches in diameter. Keep the hole in the center as open as possible.
7. Cover and let rise for another 30 minutes before baking.
8. Preheat oven to 400°F.
9. When the dough has risen, scoop up the onions in a slotted spoon and pile on top of the ring.
10. Bake for 15 minutes, then reduce the heat to 375°F and brush the exposed surface with more olive oil.
11. Continue baking another 15 to 20 minutes until golden and crispy.
12. Remove from oven and slide onto a wire rack to cool.
